titleEnglish: Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga — The Yoga of the Vision of the Universal Form
description: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 11, Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga, is the most visually dramatic and awe-inspiring chapter in the entire Gita. Arjuna, having heard about Krishna's divine glories, requests to see His cosmic form. Krishna grants him divine vision (divya drishti) to behold the universal form (Vishwarupa) — containing all gods, beings, worlds, and the entire creation within one body. Arjuna sees countless faces, eyes, arms, and divine ornaments, a radiance exceeding a thousand suns rising simultaneously. He beholds warriors being devoured by the cosmic form and is terrified. Krishna reveals the iconic truth: 'Kalo'smi — I am Time, the destroyer of all worlds.' After this overwhelming vision, Arjuna prays for forgiveness and asks Krishna to return to His gentle four-armed form. Krishna declares that this cosmic vision cannot be attained by Vedic study or austerities — only through exclusive devotion can one truly see, know, and enter into the Divine.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
मदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम्।
यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम॥
verses.transliteration:
Mad-Anugrahaya Paramam Guhyam-Adhyatma-Samjnitam.
Yat-Tvayo-Uktam Vachas-Tena Moho'yam Vigato Mama.

wordByWord.meaning: known as the science of Self

verses.hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले — मुझ पर अनुग्रह करने के लिए आपने जो परम गोपनीय अध्यात्म-विषयक वचन कहे, उनसे मेरा यह मोह दूर हो गया है।
verses.englishTranslation: Arjuna said: By the supremely confidential spiritual words You have spoken out of compassion for me, my delusion has been dispelled.
verses.commentary: Arjuna acknowledges that Krishna's teachings on divine glories and the science of the Self have cleared his confusion. The word 'moha' (delusion) — the very problem stated at the beginning of the Gita — is now declared as gone. This verse sets the stage for Arjuna's bold request to see the cosmic form.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया।
त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Bhava-Apyayau Hi Bhutanam Shrutau Vistarasho Maya.
Tvattah Kamala-Patraksha Mahatmyam-Api Cha-Avyayam.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे कमलनयन! मैंने आपसे प्राणियों की उत्पत्ति और प्रलय विस्तारपूर्वक सुनी है तथा आपका अविनाशी माहात्म्य भी सुना है।
verses.englishTranslation: O Lotus-eyed One, I have heard from You in detail about the origin and dissolution of beings, and also Your imperishable glory.
verses.commentary: Arjuna summarizes what he has learned — the creation and dissolution of beings, and Krishna's imperishable glory. The beautiful epithet 'Kamala-Patraksha' (lotus-eyed) reveals Arjuna's devotion. Having heard the theory, he now desires direct experience.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर।
द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम॥
verses.transliteration:
Evam-Etad-Yatha-Attha Tvam-Atmanam Parameshvara.
Drashtum-Ichchhami Te Rupam-Aishvaram Purushottama.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे परमेश्वर! आपने अपने विषय में जो कुछ कहा है, वह सब ठीक वैसा ही है। हे पुरुषोत्तम! अब मैं आपके ऐश्वर्यमय रूप को देखना चाहता हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: O Supreme Lord, You are just as You have described Yourself. O Supreme Person, I now wish to see Your divine majestic form.
verses.commentary: Having accepted Krishna's words as truth, Arjuna takes the bold step of requesting direct vision of the cosmic form. This transition from hearing to seeing represents the progression from theoretical knowledge to direct realization.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुमिति प्रभो।
योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयात्मानमव्ययम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Manyase Yadi Tat-Shakyam Maya Drashtum-Iti Prabho.
Yogeshvara Tato Me Tvam Darshaya-Atmanam-Avyayam.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे प्रभो! यदि आप समझते हैं कि मेरे लिए उसे देखना सम्भव है, तो हे योगेश्वर! मुझे अपना अविनाशी स्वरूप दिखाइए।
verses.englishTranslation: O Lord, if You think it is possible for me to see that form, then O Lord of Yoga, please reveal to me Your imperishable Self.
verses.commentary: Arjuna's request is humble — 'if You think it possible' shows he defers to Krishna's judgment. The title 'Yogeshvara' acknowledges that only Krishna has the power to make such a vision possible.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
पश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः।
नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च॥
verses.transliteration:
Pashya Me Partha Rupani Shatasho'tha Sahasrashah.
Nana-Vidhani Divyani Nana-Varna-Akritini Cha.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान बोले — हे पार्थ! अब तुम मेरे सैकड़ों-हजारों नाना प्रकार के, दिव्य तथा नाना वर्णों और आकृतियों वाले रूपों को देखो।
verses.englishTranslation: The Supreme Lord said: Behold, O Partha, My forms by the hundreds and thousands — of various kinds, divine, and of various colors and shapes.
verses.commentary: Krishna responds with magnificent immediacy — 'Pashya!' (Behold!). The scale is staggering — hundreds and thousands of forms, each divine, each unique in color and shape.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
पश्यादित्यान्वसून्रुद्रानश्विनौ मरुतस्तथा।
बहून्यदृष्टपूर्वाणि पश्याश्चर्याणि भारत॥
verses.transliteration:
Pashya-Adityan-Vasun-Rudran-Ashvinau Marutas-Tatha.
Bahuny-Adrishta-Purvani Pashya-Ashcharyani Bharata.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे भारत! आदित्य, वसु, रुद्र, अश्विनीकुमार और मरुतों को देखो। बहुत-से ऐसे आश्चर्य देखो जो पहले कभी नहीं देखे गए।
verses.englishTranslation: Behold the Adityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, the twin Ashvins, and the Maruts. Behold many wonders never seen before, O Bharata.
verses.commentary: Krishna specifically names the celestial groups that Arjuna will see — the twelve Adityas, eight Vasus, eleven Rudras, twin Ashvin healers, and the Marut wind gods. The phrase 'adrishta-purvani' emphasizes the unprecedented nature of this vision.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम्।
मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद्द्रष्टुमिच्छसि॥
verses.transliteration:
Iha-Ikastham Jagat-Kritsnam Pashya-Adya Sa-Chara-Acharam.
Mama Dehe Gudakesha Yach-Cha-Anyad Drashtum-Ichchhasi.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे गुडाकेश! आज यहाँ मेरे शरीर में एक ही स्थान पर चराचर सहित सम्पूर्ण जगत को देखो और जो कुछ भी तुम देखना चाहो वह भी देखो।
verses.englishTranslation: Behold now, O Gudakesha, the entire universe — moving and unmoving — assembled here in one place in My body, and whatever else you wish to see.
verses.commentary: Krishna makes an extraordinary offer — the entire universe concentrated in His body. The phrase 'yach cha anyad' (and whatever else) is remarkably generous, allowing Arjuna to see anything he desires.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा।
दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Na Tu Mam Shakyase Drashtum-Anena-Eva Sva-Chakshusha.
Divyam Dadami Te Chakshuh Pashya Me Yogam-Aishvaram.
verses.hindiTranslation: परन्तु तुम इन अपने साधारण नेत्रों से मुझे देखने में समर्थ नहीं हो। इसलिए मैं तुम्हें दिव्य दृष्टि देता हूँ — मेरा ईश्वरीय योग देखो।
verses.englishTranslation: But you cannot see Me with your own eyes. Therefore I give you divine vision — behold My supreme mystic power.
verses.commentary: This crucial verse reveals that the cosmic form cannot be perceived with ordinary senses. Divine vision (divya drishti) is a special gift from God, representing the transforming power of divine grace.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्महायोगेश्वरो हरिः।
दर्शयामास पार्थाय परमं रूपमैश्वरम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Evam-Uktva Tato Rajan-Maha-Yogeshvaro Harih.
Darshayamasa Parthaya Paramam Rupam-Aishvaram.
verses.hindiTranslation: संजय बोले — हे राजन्! इस प्रकार कहकर महायोगेश्वर हरि ने पार्थ को अपना परम ऐश्वर्यमय रूप दिखाया।
verses.englishTranslation: Sanjaya said: O King, having spoken thus, Hari, the great Lord of Yoga, then showed Partha His supreme majestic form.
verses.commentary: Sanjaya narrates this pivotal moment to the blind King Dhritarashtra. The title 'Maha-Yogeshvara' emphasizes that only the supreme master of all mystic powers could reveal such a form.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
अनेकवक्त्रनयनमनेकाद्भुतदर्शनम्।
अनेकदिव्याभरणं दिव्यानेकोद्यतायुधम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Aneka-Vaktra-Nayanam-Aneka-Adbhuta-Darshanam.
Aneka-Divya-Abharanam Divya-Aneko-Udyata-Ayudham.
verses.hindiTranslation: उस विश्वरूप में अनेक मुख, अनेक नेत्र, अनेक अद्भुत दृश्य, अनेक दिव्य आभूषण और अनेक उठाए हुए दिव्य शस्त्र थे।
verses.englishTranslation: With countless faces and eyes, full of countless wondrous sights, adorned with countless divine ornaments, and wielding countless divine weapons raised aloft.
verses.commentary: Sanjaya begins describing the cosmic form with a series of 'aneka' (many/countless), creating a sense of limitlessness. The form has innumerable faces, eyes, ornaments, and weapons — each suggesting a different divine function.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम्।
सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Divya-Malya-Ambara-Dharam Divya-Gandha-Anulepanm.
Sarva-Ashcharya-Mayam Devam-Anantam Vishvato-Mukham.
verses.hindiTranslation: दिव्य मालाएँ और वस्त्र धारण किए हुए, दिव्य गन्ध से अनुलेपित, सब प्रकार के आश्चर्यों से पूर्ण, अनन्त और सब ओर मुखों वाले उस देव को अर्जुन ने देखा।
verses.englishTranslation: Wearing divine garlands and garments, anointed with divine fragrances — full of all wonders, infinite, with faces in every direction.
verses.commentary: The description continues with sensory richness — divine garlands, celestial garments, and heavenly fragrances. The form is 'anantam' (infinite) and 'vishvato-mukham' (facing all directions), suggesting transcendence of space.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता।
यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः॥
verses.transliteration:
Divi Surya-Sahasrasya Bhaved-Yugapad-Utthita.
Yadi Bhah Sadrishi Sa Syad-Bhasas-Tasya Maha-Atmanah.
verses.hindiTranslation: यदि आकाश में हजारों सूर्य एक साथ उदय हों, तो वह प्रकाश भी उस विश्वरूप महात्मा की आभा के समान हो सकता है।
verses.englishTranslation: If a thousand suns were to rise simultaneously in the sky, their radiance might perhaps resemble the effulgence of that great Soul.
verses.commentary: This is perhaps the most famous verse in the chapter, later quoted by J. Robert Oppenheimer. The comparison of a thousand suns rising simultaneously attempts to convey the inconceivable brilliance of the cosmic form.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा।
अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा॥
verses.transliteration:
Tatra-Ekastham Jagat-Kritsnam Pravibhaktam-Anekadha.
Apashyad-Deva-Devasya Sharire Pandavas-Tada.
verses.hindiTranslation: उस समय पाण्डुपुत्र अर्जुन ने देवदेव कृष्ण के शरीर में एक ही स्थान पर अनेक भागों में विभक्त सम्पूर्ण जगत को देखा।
verses.englishTranslation: Then the son of Pandu beheld the entire universe, with its many divisions, all gathered together in one place, in the body of the God of gods.
verses.commentary: Arjuna sees the entire universe unified within Krishna's single body. The paradox of multiplicity within unity is the essence of the cosmic vision.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः।
प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत॥
verses.transliteration:
Tatah Sa Vismaya-Avishtho Hrishta-Roma Dhananjayah.
Pranamya Shirasa Devam Krita-Anjalir-Abhashat.
verses.hindiTranslation: तब विस्मय से अभिभूत, रोमाञ्चित होकर धनञ्जय ने भगवान को शिर झुकाकर प्रणाम किया और हाथ जोड़कर बोले।
verses.englishTranslation: Then Dhananjaya (Arjuna), filled with wonder and his hair standing on end, bowed his head before the Lord and spoke with folded hands.
verses.commentary: Sanjaya describes Arjuna's physical response — hair standing on end (a sign of overwhelming awe), bowing down, and joining palms in reverence. The mighty warrior is completely overwhelmed by divine majesty.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
पश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहे
सर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान्।
ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थम्
ऋषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान्॥
verses.transliteration:
Pashyami Devams-Tava Deva Dehe
Sarvams-Tatha Bhuta-Vishesha-Sanghan.
Brahmanam-Isham Kamala-Asana-Stham
Rishimsh-Cha Sarvan-Uragamsh-Cha Divyan.
verses.hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले — हे देव! मैं आपके शरीर में सब देवताओं को, प्राणियों के विशेष समूहों को, कमल पर विराजमान ब्रह्मा जी को, शिव जी को, सब ऋषियों को और दिव्य नागों को देख रहा हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Arjuna said: O Lord, I see in Your body all the gods, various groups of special beings, Lord Brahma seated on the lotus, Lord Shiva, all the sages, and the celestial serpents.
verses.commentary: Arjuna begins describing the cosmic vision. Both Brahma and Shiva are contained within Krishna's form, establishing Krishna's supreme position above the entire Hindu pantheon.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं
पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम्।
नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं
पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप॥
verses.transliteration:
Aneka-Bahu-Udara-Vaktra-Netram
Pashyami Tvam Sarvato'nanta-Rupam.
Na-Antam Na Madhyam Na Punas-Tava-Adim
Pashyami Vishveshvara Vishva-Rupa.
verses.hindiTranslation: अनेक भुजाओं, उदरों, मुखों और नेत्रों वाले, सब ओर अनन्त रूप वाले आपको मैं देख रहा हूँ। हे विश्वेश्वर! हे विश्वरूप! मैं न आपका अन्त देखता हूँ, न मध्य, न आदि।
verses.englishTranslation: I see You with countless arms, stomachs, faces, and eyes — Your infinite form on every side. I see no end, no middle, and no beginning of You, O Lord of the Universe, O Universal Form.
verses.commentary: Arjuna describes the boundlessness of the cosmic form — it has no beginning, middle, or end. The form literally fills all of space and time.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च
तेजोराशिं सर्वतो दीप्तिमन्तम्।
पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ताद्
दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Kiritinam Gadinam Chakrinam Cha
Tejo-Rashim Sarvato Diptimantam.
Pashyami Tvam Dur-Nirikshyam Samantad
Dipta-Anala-Arka-Dyutim-Aprameyam.

wordByWord.meaning: brilliance like blazing fire and sun

verses.hindiTranslation: मुकुटधारी, गदाधारी, चक्रधारी, तेज के पुंज, सब ओर से प्रकाशमान, प्रज्वलित अग्नि और सूर्य की-सी द्युति वाले, कठिनता से देखे जा सकने वाले अप्रमेय आपको मैं देख रहा हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: I see You wearing a crown, bearing a mace and discus, a mass of radiance blazing on all sides — difficult to look at, with the brilliance of blazing fire and sun, immeasurable.
verses.commentary: The form carries the traditional Vishnu symbols — crown, mace, and discus — but on an unimaginably vast scale. The radiance is so intense that it is hard to gaze upon.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं
त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।
त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता
सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे॥
verses.transliteration:
Tvam-Aksharam Paramam Veditavyam
Tvam-Asya Vishvasya Param Nidhanam.
Tvam-Avyayah Shashvata-Dharma-Gopta
Sanatanas-Tvam Purusho Mato Me.
verses.hindiTranslation: आप परम अक्षर, जानने योग्य परम तत्त्व हैं, इस विश्व के परम आश्रय हैं, अव्यय, सनातन धर्म के रक्षक हैं — मेरी दृष्टि में आप सनातन पुरुष हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: You are the Imperishable, the Supreme to be known. You are the ultimate resting place of this universe. You are the unchanging protector of eternal dharma. You are the Eternal Supreme Person.
verses.commentary: Arjuna declares what the cosmic vision reveals. Krishna is the imperishable reality, the ultimate shelter, and the guardian of eternal dharma.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्यम्
अनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम्।
पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रं
स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Anadi-Madhya-Antam-Ananta-Viryam
Ananta-Bahum Shashi-Surya-Netram.
Pashyami Tvam Dipta-Hutasha-Vaktram
Sva-Tejasa Vishvam-Idam Tapantam.
verses.hindiTranslation: आदि, मध्य और अन्त से रहित, अनन्त वीर्य वाले, अनन्त भुजाओं वाले, चन्द्र-सूर्य रूपी नेत्रों वाले, प्रज्वलित अग्नि जैसे मुख वाले और अपने तेज से विश्व को तपाते हुए आपको मैं देख रहा हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: I see You without beginning, middle, or end — of infinite power, with infinite arms, the moon and sun as Your eyes, Your mouth like blazing fire, scorching this universe with Your own radiance.
verses.commentary: The sun and moon serve as Krishna's eyes, His mouth blazes like sacrificial fire, and His radiance heats the entire universe. This is a form that transcends time itself.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हि
व्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः।
दृष्ट्वाद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदं
लोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन्॥
verses.transliteration:
Dyava-Prithivyor-Idam-Antaram Hi
Vyaptam Tvaya-Ikena Dishah-Cha Sarvah.
Drishtva-Adbhutam Rupam-Ugram Tavedam
Loka-Trayam Pravyathitam Mahatman.
verses.hindiTranslation: स्वर्ग और पृथ्वी के बीच का समस्त अन्तर और सब दिशाएँ अकेले आपसे व्याप्त हैं। आपके इस अद्भुत और भयंकर रूप को देखकर तीनों लोक भयभीत हो रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: The space between heaven and earth and all directions are pervaded by You alone. Seeing this wondrous and terrible form of Yours, O Great Soul, the three worlds tremble with fear.
verses.commentary: The word 'ugram' (terrible) marks a shift — the vision is no longer just beautiful but also terrifying. The three worlds tremble, showing that the cosmic form inspires both awe and fear.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घा विशन्ति
केचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति।
स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घाः
स्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ami Hi Tvam Sura-Sangha Vishanti
Kechid-Bhitah Pranjalayo Grinanti.
Svasti-Ity-Uktva Maharshi-Siddha-Sanghah
Stuvanti Tvam Stutibhih Pushkalabhih.

wordByWord.meaning: hosts of great sages and Siddhas

verses.hindiTranslation: देवताओं के समूह आपमें प्रवेश कर रहे हैं, कुछ भयभीत होकर हाथ जोड़े प्रार्थना कर रहे हैं। महर्षि और सिद्धों के समूह 'स्वस्ति' कहकर उत्तम स्तुतियों से आपकी स्तुति कर रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Hosts of gods enter into You; some, frightened, pray with folded hands. Groups of great sages and perfected beings cry 'Svasti!' and praise You with abundant hymns.
verses.commentary: The reaction of celestial beings is dramatic — some gods enter into the form, others pray in fear, while sages and Siddhas offer hymns of praise.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
रुद्रादित्या वसवो ये च साध्या
विश्वेऽश्विनौ मरुतश्चोष्मपाश्च।
गन्धर्वयक्षासुरसिद्धसङ्घा
वीक्षन्ते त्वां विस्मिताश्चैव सर्वे॥
verses.transliteration:
Rudra-Aditya Vasavo Ye Cha Sadhya
Vishve'shvinau Marutash-Cho-Ushma-Pash-Cha.
Gandharva-Yaksha-Asura-Siddha-Sangha
Vikshante Tvam Vismitash-Cha-Eva Sarve.

wordByWord.meaning: hosts of Gandharvas, Yakshas, Asuras, Siddhas

verses.hindiTranslation: रुद्र, आदित्य, वसु, साध्य, विश्वेदेव, अश्विनीकुमार, मरुत, पितर, गन्धर्व, यक्ष, असुर और सिद्धों के समूह — सब आश्चर्यचकित होकर आपको देख रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: The Rudras, Adityas, Vasus, Sadhyas, Vishvadevas, Ashvins, Maruts, ancestors, Gandharvas, Yakshas, Asuras, and Siddhas — all gaze at You in amazement.
verses.commentary: Every category of celestial being stands astonished, emphasizing that the cosmic form transcends the understanding of all beings.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रं
महाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम्।
बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालं
दृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Rupam Mahat-Te Bahu-Vaktra-Netram
Maha-Baho Bahu-Bahu-Uru-Padam.
Bahu-Udaram Bahu-Damshtra-Karalam
Drishtva Lokah Pravyathitas-Tatha-Aham.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे महाबाहो! आपके इस महान रूप को — जिसमें अनेक मुख, नेत्र, बाहु, जंघा, पैर, उदर और भयंकर दाढ़ें हैं — देखकर सब लोक और मैं स्वयं भी भयभीत हो रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: O Mighty-armed, seeing Your great form with many faces, eyes, arms, thighs, feet, stomachs, and many terrible fangs — the worlds are frightened, and so am I.
verses.commentary: The 'bahu-damshtra-karalam' (many terrible fangs) transforms the form from majestic to fearsome. Arjuna honestly admits his own fear alongside the world's.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
नभःस्पृशं दीप्तमनेकवर्णं
व्यात्ताननं दीप्तविशालनेत्रम्।
दृष्ट्वा हि त्वां प्रव्यथितान्तरात्मा
धृतिं न विन्दामि शमं च विष्णो॥
verses.transliteration:
Nabhah-Sprisham Diptam-Aneka-Varnam
Vyatta-Ananam Dipta-Vishala-Netram.
Drishtva Hi Tvam Pravyathita-Antaratma
Dhritim Na Vindami Shamam Cha Vishno.
verses.hindiTranslation: आकाश को छूते हुए, देदीप्यमान, खुले हुए मुख वाले, चमकते हुए विशाल नेत्रों वाले आपको देखकर मेरा अन्तरात्मा भयभीत हो गया है। हे विष्णो! मैं न धैर्य पा रहा, न शान्ति।
verses.englishTranslation: Seeing You touching the sky, blazing with many colors, mouths wide open, with blazing huge eyes — my inner soul trembles. I find neither courage nor peace, O Vishnu.
verses.commentary: The open mouths, blazing eyes, and sky-touching form have shaken Arjuna to his core. Even calling out 'O Vishnu' is a plea for comfort.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि
दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि।
दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास॥
verses.transliteration:
Damshtra-Karalani Cha Te Mukhani
Drishtva-Eva Kala-Anala-Sannibhani.
Disho Na Jane Na Labhe Cha Sharma
Prasida Devesha Jagan-Nivasa.
verses.hindiTranslation: आपके भयंकर दाढ़ों वाले मुखों को — जो प्रलयकाल की अग्नि जैसे दिख रहे हैं — देखकर मैं दिशाओं को नहीं पहचान पाता और मुझे सुख नहीं मिल रहा। हे देवेश! हे जगन्निवास! प्रसन्न होइए।
verses.englishTranslation: Seeing Your faces with terrible fangs, blazing like the fire of cosmic dissolution, I lose my sense of direction and find no comfort. Be gracious, O Lord of gods, O Refuge of the universe.
verses.commentary: Arjuna is completely disoriented — he cannot tell which direction is which. His plea 'Prasida' (be gracious) is a desperate cry for mercy.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः
सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः।
भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथासौ
सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ami Cha Tvam Dhritarashtrasya Putrah
Sarve Saha-Eva-Avani-Pala-Sanghaih.
Bhishmo Dronah Suta-Putras-Tatha-Asau
Saha-Asmadiyair-Api Yodha-Mukhyaih.
verses.hindiTranslation: धृतराष्ट्र के सब पुत्र राजाओं के समूह सहित, भीष्म, द्रोण और कर्ण तथा हमारी ओर के भी प्रमुख योद्धा —
verses.englishTranslation: All the sons of Dhritarashtra, along with hosts of kings, Bhishma, Drona, Karna, and the chief warriors on our side too —
verses.commentary: Arjuna sees specific warriors from both armies in the cosmic vision. The inclusion of Pandava warriors shows the cosmic form's destructive aspect is impartial.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
वक्त्राणि ते त्वरमाणा विशन्ति
दंष्ट्राकरालानि भयानकानि।
केचिद्विलग्ना दशनान्तरेषु
सन्दृश्यन्ते चूर्णितैरुत्तमाङ्गैः॥
verses.transliteration:
Vaktrani Te Tvaramana Vishanti
Damshtra-Karalani Bhayanakani.
Kechid-Vilagna Dashana-Antareshu
Sandrishyante Churnitair-Uttama-Angaih.
verses.hindiTranslation: ये सब भयंकर दाढ़ों वाले आपके मुखों में तेजी से प्रवेश कर रहे हैं। कुछ दाँतों के बीच में फँसे हुए चूर्ण किए हुए सिरों के साथ दिखाई दे रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: They rush into Your fearsome mouths with terrible fangs. Some are seen caught between Your teeth, their heads crushed to powder.
verses.commentary: This terrifying vision shows warriors being drawn into the cosmic mouths and crushed. It reveals Time as the great devourer of all beings.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाः
समुद्रमेवाभिमुखा द्रवन्ति।
तथा तवामी नरलोकवीरा
विशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति॥
verses.transliteration:
Yatha Nadinam Bahavo'mbu-Vegah
Samudram-Eva-Abhimukha Dravanti.
Tatha Tava-Ami Nara-Loka-Vira
Vishanti Vaktrany-Abhivijvalanti.
verses.hindiTranslation: जैसे नदियों के अनेक जलप्रवाह समुद्र की ओर दौड़ते हैं, वैसे ही ये मनुष्यलोक के वीर आपके प्रज्वलित मुखों में प्रवेश कर रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: As many torrents of rivers flow toward the ocean, so do these heroes of the human world rush into Your blazing mouths.
verses.commentary: The simile of rivers flowing inevitably into the ocean conveys the helplessness of all beings before the force of Time and destiny.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गा
विशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः।
तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोकास्
तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Yatha Pradiptam Jvalanam Patanga
Vishanti Nashaya Samriddha-Vegah.
Tatha-Eva Nashaya Vishanti Lokas-
Tava-Api Vaktrani Samriddha-Vegah.
verses.hindiTranslation: जैसे पतंगे तीव्र वेग से प्रज्वलित अग्नि में अपने विनाश के लिए प्रवेश करते हैं, वैसे ही ये लोग भी तीव्र वेग से आपके मुखों में अपने विनाश के लिए प्रवेश कर रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: As moths rush with great speed into a blazing fire for their destruction, so do these beings rush with great speed into Your mouths for their destruction.
verses.commentary: The moth-and-flame simile highlights the tragic irony — beings hasten toward their own end, unable to resist the attraction of their destiny.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
लेलिह्यसे ग्रसमानः समन्ताल्
लोकान्समग्रान्वदनैर्ज्वलद्भिः।
तेजोभिरापूर्य जगत्समग्रं
भासस्तवोग्राः प्रतपन्ति विष्णो॥
verses.transliteration:
Lelihyase Grasamanah Samantat
Lokan-Samgran-Vadanair-Jvaladbhih.
Tejobhir-Apurya Jagat-Samagram
Bhasas-Tavo-Ugrah Pratapanti Vishno.
verses.hindiTranslation: आप प्रज्वलित मुखों से सब लोकों को चारों ओर से निगल रहे हैं और चाट रहे हैं। हे विष्णो! आपकी उग्र किरणें सारे जगत को तपा रही हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: You lick and devour all the worlds from every side with Your blazing mouths. O Vishnu, Your fierce rays fill the entire universe with radiance and scorch it.
verses.commentary: The most intense moment — the cosmic form actively consuming the worlds. Even the benign sustainer Vishnu has this all-consuming aspect.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपो
नमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद।
विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यं
न हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Akhyahi Me Ko Bhavan-Ugra-Rupo
Namo'stu Te Devavara Prasida.
Vijnatam-Ichchhami Bhavantam-Adyam
Na Hi Prajanami Tava Pravrittim.
verses.hindiTranslation: मुझे बताइए कि उग्र रूप वाले आप कौन हैं? हे देवश्रेष्ठ! आपको नमस्कार, प्रसन्न होइए। मैं आपको जानना चाहता हूँ, क्योंकि मैं आपकी प्रवृत्ति नहीं समझ पा रहा।
verses.englishTranslation: Tell me, who are You in this fierce form? Salutations to You, O best of gods, be gracious. I wish to know You, the Primal One, for I do not understand Your purpose.
verses.commentary: Arjuna asks 'Who are You?' — the fierce cosmic form seems like an entirely different being. This question directly prompts the Gita's most iconic response.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो
लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः।
ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे
येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Kalo'smi Loka-Kshaya-Krit Pravruddho
Lokan-Samahartum-Iha Pravrittah.
Rite'pi Tvam Na Bhavishyanti Sarve
Ye'vasthitah Praty-Anikeshu Yodhah.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान बोले — मैं लोकों का संहार करने वाला प्रबल काल हूँ। इस समय इन लोकों को नष्ट करने में प्रवृत्त हूँ। तेरे बिना भी प्रतिपक्ष के सब योद्धा नहीं रहेंगे।
verses.englishTranslation: The Supreme Lord said: I am mighty Time, the destroyer of worlds, now engaged in destroying them. Even without you, all the warriors in the opposing armies shall cease to exist.
verses.commentary: This is the most iconic verse of the Gita — 'Kalo'smi.' Krishna reveals that as Time, He is the ultimate destroyer. The warriors are already doomed. This liberates Arjuna from guilt, showing death is ordained by cosmic Time.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व
जित्वा शत्रून्भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्।
मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव
निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन्॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasmat-Tvam-Uttishtha Yasho Labhasva
Jitva Shatrun Bhunkshva Rajyam Samriddham.
Maya-Eva-Ete Nihatah Purvam-Eva
Nimitta-Matram Bhava Savya-Sachin.
verses.hindiTranslation: इसलिए तुम उठो, यश प्राप्त करो, शत्रुओं को जीतकर समृद्ध राज्य भोगो। ये सब मेरे द्वारा पहले से ही मारे जा चुके हैं। हे सव्यसाचिन्! तुम केवल निमित्तमात्र बनो।
verses.englishTranslation: Therefore arise, win glory, conquer your enemies, and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. These warriors have already been slain by Me. Be merely an instrument, O Savyasachin.
verses.commentary: This resolves Arjuna's moral dilemma — he is not the true doer but an instrument of divine will. The call to 'arise' echoes the Gita's message of righteous action.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च
कर्णं तथान्यानपि योधवीरान्।
मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा
युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान्॥
verses.transliteration:
Dronam Cha Bhishmam Cha Jayadratham Cha
Karnam Tatha-Anyan-Api Yodha-Viran.
Maya Hatams-Tvam Jahi Ma Vyathishtha
Yudhyasva Jetasi Rane Sapatnan.
verses.hindiTranslation: द्रोण, भीष्म, जयद्रथ, कर्ण और अन्य वीर योद्धा — इन्हें मैंने पहले से ही मार दिया है। तुम इन्हें मारो, विचलित मत होओ। युद्ध करो, तुम शत्रुओं को जीतोगे।
verses.englishTranslation: Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna, and other brave warriors have already been slain by Me. Slay them, do not be distressed. Fight, and you shall conquer your enemies in battle.
verses.commentary: Krishna names the most formidable opponents and assures they are already dead in the divine plan. The command 'ma vyathishthah' directly answers Arjuna's original distress.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्य
कृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी।
नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णं
सगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य॥
verses.transliteration:
Etat-Shrutva Vachanam Keshavasya
Krita-Anjalir-Vepamanah Kiriti.
Namaskritva Bhuya Eva-Aha Krishnam
Sa-Gadgadam Bhita-Bhitah Pranamya.
verses.hindiTranslation: संजय बोले — केशव का यह वचन सुनकर किरीटी (अर्जुन) काँपते हुए हाथ जोड़कर, नमस्कार करके, अत्यन्त भयभीत होकर, गद्गद वाणी से कृष्ण से पुनः बोला।
verses.englishTranslation: Sanjaya said: Having heard these words of Keshava, the crowned Arjuna, trembling with folded hands, bowed down and spoke again to Krishna in a choked voice, greatly frightened.
verses.commentary: Sanjaya describes Arjuna's physical state — trembling, voice choked with emotion, deeply frightened. The mighty warrior is reduced to this state by the cosmic vision.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
स्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्या
जगत्प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च।
रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्ति
सर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्घाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Sthane Hrishikesha Tava Prakirtya
Jagat-Prahrishyaty-Anurajyate Cha.
Rakshamsi Bhitani Disho Dravanti
Sarve Namasyanti Cha Siddha-Sanghah.
verses.hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले — हे हृषीकेश! यह उचित ही है कि आपकी कीर्ति से जगत हर्षित और अनुरक्त हो रहा है, राक्षस भयभीत होकर भाग रहे हैं और सिद्धों के समूह नमस्कार कर रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Arjuna said: It is right, O Hrishikesha, that the world rejoices and is devoted to Your praise. Demons flee in fear in all directions, and all the hosts of Siddhas bow before You.
verses.commentary: Arjuna begins his prayer of praise, acknowledging that the world's dual response — devotion from the righteous and terror among the wicked — is fitting.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन्महात्मन्
गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे।
अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवास
त्वमक्षरं सदसत्तत्परं यत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Kasmach-Cha Te Na Nameran-Mahatman
Gariyase Brahmano'py-Adi-Kartre.
Ananta Devesha Jagan-Nivasa
Tvam-Aksharam Sad-Asat-Tat-Param Yat.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे महात्मन्! ब्रह्मा के भी आदि कर्ता, सबसे बड़े आपको वे क्यों नमस्कार न करें? आप अक्षर हैं, सत् और असत् तथा उनसे भी परे जो है — वह आप हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: And why should they not bow to You, O Great Soul — who are greater even than Brahma? O Infinite One, O Lord of gods — You are the Imperishable, the being, the non-being, and that which is beyond both.
verses.commentary: Krishna is being (sat), non-being (asat), and that which transcends both. This places Krishna beyond all categories of existence.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराणस्
त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।
वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम
त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप॥
verses.transliteration:
Tvam-Adi-Devah Purushah Puranah
Tvam-Asya Vishvasya Param Nidhanam.
Vetta-Asi Vedyam Cha Param Cha Dhama
Tvaya Tatam Vishvam-Ananta-Rupa.

wordByWord.meaning: that which is to be known

verses.hindiTranslation: आप आदिदेव, सनातन पुरुष हैं, इस विश्व के परम आश्रय हैं। आप ज्ञाता, ज्ञेय और परमधाम हैं। हे अनन्तरूप! आपसे यह सम्पूर्ण विश्व व्याप्त है।
verses.englishTranslation: You are the Primal God, the Ancient Person, the supreme resting place. You are the Knower, the Known, and the Supreme Abode. The universe is pervaded by You, O Infinite Form.
verses.commentary: As Knower, Known, and Supreme Abode, Krishna encompasses the subject, object, and goal of all knowledge.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः
प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च।
नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः
पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते॥
verses.transliteration:
Vayur-Yamo'gnir-Varunah Shashankah
Prajapatish-Tvam Prapitamahash-Cha.
Namo Namas-Te'stu Sahasra-Kritvah
Punash-Cha Bhuyo'pi Namo Namas-Te.
verses.hindiTranslation: आप वायु, यम, अग्नि, वरुण, चन्द्रमा, प्रजापति और प्रपितामह हैं। आपको हजारों बार नमस्कार! फिर से, और फिर से नमस्कार!
verses.englishTranslation: You are Vayu, Yama, Agni, Varuna, the Moon, Prajapati, and the great-grandfather. Salutations to You a thousand times, and again and again — salutations to You!
verses.commentary: The repetition of 'namo namah' a thousand times and then again expresses devotion that cannot be satisfied by any finite worship.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्ते
नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व।
अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वं
सर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः॥
verses.transliteration:
Namah Purastad-Atha Prishthataste
Namo'stu Te Sarvata Eva Sarva.
Ananta-Virya-Amita-Vikramas-Tvam
Sarvam Samapnoshi Tato'si Sarvah.
verses.hindiTranslation: सामने से, पीछे से, सब ओर से आपको नमस्कार! हे सर्वस्वरूप! आप सबको व्याप्त करते हैं, इसलिए आप ही सर्वस्व हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Salutations from the front, from behind, and from all sides, O All! You pervade everything, and therefore You are everything.
verses.commentary: The logical conclusion 'You pervade all, therefore You are all' is a profound philosophical statement moving from omnipresence to divine identity with everything.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं
हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति।
अजानता महिमानं तवेदं
मया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि॥
verses.transliteration:
Sakhe-Ti Matva Prasabham Yad-Uktam
He Krishna He Yadava He Sakhe-Ti.
Ajanata Mahimanam Tavedam
Maya Pramadat-Pranayena Va-Api.
verses.hindiTranslation: आपको सखा मानकर जो मैंने ढिठाई से 'हे कृष्ण', 'हे यादव', 'हे सखे' कहा — आपकी महिमा को न जानते हुए, प्रमादवश या प्रेमवश —
verses.englishTranslation: Whatever I have said carelessly, thinking of You as a friend — 'O Krishna,' 'O Yadava,' 'O friend' — not knowing this greatness of Yours, out of negligence or affection —
verses.commentary: Arjuna realizes the enormity of his casual familiarity with the Supreme Being. This tension between divine majesty and personal intimacy is one of the Gita's most touching moments.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
यच्चावहासार्थमसत्कृतोऽसि
विहारशय्यासनभोजनेषु।
एकोऽथवाप्यच्युत तत्समक्षं
तत्क्षामये त्वामहमप्रमेयम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Yach-Cha-Avahasa-Artham-Asat-Krito'si
Vihara-Shayya-Asana-Bhojaneshu.
Eko'tha-Va-Apy-Achyuta Tat-Samaksham
Tat-Kshamaye Tvam-Aham-Aprameyam.
verses.hindiTranslation: और हँसी-मजाक में, खेलते, लेटते, बैठते, भोजन करते समय जो आपका अपमान किया — अकेले में या दूसरों के सामने — हे अच्युत! उसके लिए मैं आपसे क्षमा माँगता हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: And for whatever disrespect in jest — while playing, resting, sitting, or eating — whether alone or before others, O Achyuta, I seek Your forgiveness, O Immeasurable One.
verses.commentary: Arjuna recalls specific instances of casual behavior that now feel like offenses against the Supreme Lord. This beautifully captures the struggle between personal love and divine reverence.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्य
त्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान्।
न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्यो
लोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव॥
verses.transliteration:
Pita-Asi Lokasya Chara-Acharasya
Tvam-Asya Pujyash-Cha Gurur-Gariyaan.
Na Tvat-Samo'sty-Abhyadhikah Kuto'nyo
Loka-Traye'py-Apratima-Prabhava.
verses.hindiTranslation: आप इस चराचर जगत के पिता, पूज्य और सबसे बड़े गुरु हैं। तीनों लोकों में आपके समान भी कोई नहीं, तो आपसे बढ़कर कोई कहाँ?
verses.englishTranslation: You are the father of this world, its most venerable teacher, the greatest. There is none equal to You in the three worlds — how then could there be one greater? O Lord of incomparable power!
verses.commentary: Arjuna establishes Krishna's absolute supremacy through logic — He is the father and teacher of all creation. Since no one equals Him, the question of anyone being greater does not arise.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायं
प्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम्।
पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युः
प्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasmat-Pranamya Pranidhaya Kayam
Prasadaye Tvam-Aham-Isham-Idyam.
Pita-Iva Putrasya Sakhe-Iva Sakhyuh
Priyah Priyaya-Arhasi Deva Sodhum.
verses.hindiTranslation: इसलिए शरीर झुकाकर प्रणाम करके, प्रशंसनीय ईश्वर, आपसे मैं प्रसन्नता की याचना करता हूँ। जैसे पिता पुत्र को, मित्र मित्र को, प्रियजन प्रियतम को क्षमा करता है — वैसे हे देव! आप मुझे क्षमा करें।
verses.englishTranslation: Therefore prostrating, I bow and seek Your grace, O praiseworthy Lord. As a father forgives a son, a friend a friend, a lover the beloved — please, O Lord, forgive me.
verses.commentary: Arjuna invokes three relationships — father-son, friend-friend, lover-beloved — each representing a different kind of forgiveness. God forgives through love in all its forms.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
अदृष्टपूर्वं हृषितोऽस्मि दृष्ट्वा
भयेन च प्रव्यथितं मनो मे।
तदेव मे दर्शय देवरूपं
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास॥
verses.transliteration:
Adrishta-Purvam Hrishito'smi Drishtva
Bhayena Cha Pravyathitam Mano Me.
Tad-Eva Me Darshaya Deva-Rupam
Prasida Devesha Jagan-Nivasa.
verses.hindiTranslation: पहले कभी न देखे हुए इस रूप को देखकर मैं हर्षित भी हूँ, परन्तु भय से मेरा मन व्याकुल भी है। हे देवेश! कृपा कीजिए, मुझे वही सौम्य देवरूप दिखाइए।
verses.englishTranslation: I am delighted to have seen what was never seen before, yet my mind is distressed with fear. O Lord of gods, O Refuge of the universe, be gracious — show me that gentle divine form.
verses.commentary: Arjuna experiences contradictory emotions — joy at the unprecedented vision and terror at its fearsome aspect. He requests the gentle, approachable form.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्तम्
इच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव।
तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन
सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते॥
verses.transliteration:
Kiritinam Gadinam Chakra-Hastam
Ichchhami Tvam Drashtum-Aham Tatha-Eva.
Tena-Eva Rupena Chatur-Bhujena
Sahasra-Baho Bhava Vishva-Murte.
verses.hindiTranslation: मुकुटधारी, गदाधारी, चक्रधारी — उसी चतुर्भुज रूप में मैं आपको देखना चाहता हूँ। हे सहस्रबाहो! हे विश्वमूर्ते! उसी रूप को धारण कीजिए।
verses.englishTranslation: I wish to see You as before — with crown, mace, and discus — in that same four-armed form. O thousand-armed One, O Universal Form, please assume that form.
verses.commentary: Arjuna specifically requests the four-armed Vishnu form — majestic yet approachable. This shows the human need for a personal, relatable God.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं
रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात्।
तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं
यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Maya Prasannena Tava-Arjuna-Idam
Rupam Param Darshitam-Atma-Yogat.
Tejo-Mayam Vishvam-Anantam-Adyam
Yan-Me Tvad-Anyena Na Drishta-Purvam.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान बोले — हे अर्जुन! प्रसन्न होकर मैंने अपनी योगशक्ति से तुम्हें यह परम, तेजोमय, विश्वव्यापी, अनन्त, आदि रूप दिखाया, जो तुम्हारे अतिरिक्त किसी ने पहले नहीं देखा।
verses.englishTranslation: The Supreme Lord said: O Arjuna, being pleased with you, I have shown you by My own yogic power this supreme, radiant, universal, infinite, primal form — which no one before you has ever seen.
verses.commentary: Krishna confirms that the cosmic vision was uniquely granted to Arjuna out of divine pleasure. Such revelations are acts of divine grace.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानैर्
न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः।
एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके
द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर॥
verses.transliteration:
Na Veda-Yajna-Adhyayanair-Na Danair
Na Cha Kriyabhir-Na Tapobhir-Ugraih.
Evam-Rupah Shakya Aham Nri-Loke
Drashtum Tvad-Anyena Kuru-Pravira.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे कुरुप्रवीर! न वेद-अध्ययन से, न यज्ञों से, न दान से, न क्रियाओं से, न उग्र तपस्याओं से — इस रूप में मैं मनुष्यलोक में तुम्हारे सिवा किसी और द्वारा देखा जा सकता हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Neither by Vedic study, sacrifices, charity, rituals, nor severe austerities can I be seen in this form in the human world by anyone other than you, O best of Kuru warriors.
verses.commentary: All conventional religious practices fall short. Only exclusive devotion, which Arjuna embodies, can earn this vision.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो
दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम्।
व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वं
तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य॥
verses.transliteration:
Ma Te Vyatha Ma Cha Vimudha-Bhavo
Drishtva Rupam Ghoram-Idrik-Mama-Idam.
Vyapeta-Bhih Prita-Manah Punas-Tvam
Tad-Eva Me Rupam-Idam Prapashya.
verses.hindiTranslation: मेरे इस भयंकर रूप को देखकर तुम्हें न व्यथा हो, न मोह। निर्भय होकर, प्रसन्नचित्त होकर, पुनः मेरे उसी सौम्य रूप को देखो।
verses.englishTranslation: Do not be distressed or bewildered by this terrible form of Mine. Free from fear, with a pleased mind, behold once again My familiar form.
verses.commentary: Krishna comforts Arjuna with tender words. After revealing the cosmic truth, God gently returns the devotee to a state of peace and comfort.

verses.speaker: Sanjaya
verses.devanagari:
इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा
स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः।
आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं
भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा॥
verses.transliteration:
Ity-Arjunam Vasudevas-Tatha-Uktva
Svakam Rupam Darshayamasa Bhuyah.
Ashvasayamasa Cha Bhitam-Enam
Bhutva Punah Saumya-Vapur-Mahatma.
verses.hindiTranslation: संजय बोले — वासुदेव ने ऐसा कहकर पुनः अपना सौम्य रूप दिखाया और महात्मा ने सौम्य शरीर धारण करके भयभीत अर्जुन को आश्वस्त किया।
verses.englishTranslation: Sanjaya said: Having spoken thus, Vasudeva showed His own form again. The great-souled One, assuming His gentle form, consoled the frightened Arjuna.
verses.commentary: Krishna withdraws the cosmic form and reassumes His gentle, familiar appearance. The act of consoling shows the tenderness of the Divine.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तव सौम्यं जनार्दन।
इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः॥
verses.transliteration:
Drishtva-Idam Manusham Rupam Tava Saumyam Janardana.
Idanim-Asmi Samvrittah Sa-Chetah Prakritim Gatah.
verses.hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले — हे जनार्दन! आपके इस सौम्य मानवीय रूप को देखकर अब मैं स्थिर-चित्त हो गया हूँ, सचेत होकर अपनी स्वाभाविक स्थिति में लौट आया हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Arjuna said: O Janardana, seeing this gentle human form of Yours, I am now composed. My mind is restored, and I have returned to my natural state.
verses.commentary: Arjuna expresses relief at seeing Krishna's familiar form. The human form of God allows the devotee to relate, love, and serve.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम।
देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः॥
verses.transliteration:
Su-Dur-Darsham-Idam Rupam Drishtavan-Asi Yan-Mama.
Deva Apy-Asya Rupasya Nityam Darshana-Kankshinah.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान बोले — मेरा जो यह रूप तुमने देखा, इसे देखना अत्यन्त कठिन है। देवता भी इस रूप के दर्शन की सदा कामना करते रहते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: The Supreme Lord said: This form which you have seen is very difficult to behold. Even the gods constantly yearn for a vision of this form.
verses.commentary: Even the gods perpetually desire to see this form but cannot. Arjuna's experience was an extraordinary act of grace.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया।
शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा॥
verses.transliteration:
Na-Aham Vedair-Na Tapasa Na Danena Na Cha-Ijyaya.
Shakya Evam-Vidho Drashtum Drishtavan-Asi Mam Yatha.
verses.hindiTranslation: न वेदों से, न तपस्या से, न दान से, न यज्ञ से मैं इस प्रकार देखा जा सकता हूँ, जैसा तुमने मुझे देखा।
verses.englishTranslation: Neither by the Vedas, nor by austerity, charity, nor sacrifice can I be seen in this form as you have seen Me.
verses.commentary: Conventional means of spiritual advancement are all insufficient for this supreme vision. Something beyond all these is required — revealed in the next verse.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन।
ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परन्तप॥
verses.transliteration:
Bhaktya Tv-Ananyaya Shakya Aham-Evam-Vidho'rjuna.
Jnatum Drashtum Cha Tattvena Praveshtum Cha Parantapa.
verses.hindiTranslation: परन्तु हे परन्तप अर्जुन! अनन्य भक्ति के द्वारा ही इस रूप में मुझे जाना, देखा और तत्त्वतः प्रवेश किया जा सकता है।
verses.englishTranslation: But by exclusive devotion alone, O Arjuna, can I be known, seen, and entered into in truth, O scorcher of foes.
verses.commentary: The climactic revelation — only through ananya bhakti (exclusive devotion) can one truly know, see, and enter into God. This makes bhakti the supreme path.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः।
निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव॥
verses.transliteration:
Mat-Karma-Krin-Mat-Paramo Mad-Bhaktah Sanga-Varjitah.
Nirvairah Sarva-Bhuteshu Yah Sa Mam-Eti Pandava.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे पाण्डव! जो मेरे लिए कर्म करता है, मुझे ही परम लक्ष्य मानता है, मेरा भक्त है, आसक्ति से रहित है और सब प्राणियों के प्रति वैरभाव से मुक्त है — वह मुझे प्राप्त होता है।
verses.englishTranslation: O Pandava, he who works for Me, regards Me as the Supreme, is devoted to Me, free from attachment, and bears no enmity toward any being — he comes to Me.
verses.commentary: The chapter's essence — a five-fold formula: (1) work for God; (2) make God the supreme goal; (3) be devoted; (4) be free from attachment; (5) harbor no enmity. This bridges karma, bhakti, and jnana yoga.
